Turn 20 ‚ÄĒ compactness gluing closes the formal gap I re-read the three attached files only. The problem statement defines F=P‚čäG, with P the free G-operator pro-2 group on x0,x1, the relators rS,rH, and the quotients őďS,őďH.

The requested conclusion is precisely an isomorphism ő¶:őďS‚ÜíőďH commuting with the canonical projections to G.

The conclusion of Turn 20 is:őďS‚ČÖGőďH.The point of this turn is to repair and sharpen the last formal step from Turn 19. Turn 19 stated a stage-by-stage Nielsen‚ÄďHensel lemma; that is morally right, but a little too strong in the form ‚Äúany stage-n solution lifts monotonically to stage n+1.‚ÄĚ The history itself shows why one should not rely on that stronger statement: the degree-16 obstruction was not killed inside a fixed high-gauge torsor, but only after retuning the lower I9 -base by the Y0‚Ü¶Y0Y4 deformation.So the correct final gluing argument is not ‚Äúevery chosen finite lift extends.‚ÄĚ It is:for every finite Magnus level N, there exists some finite-level twisted over-G Nielsen solution; the finite-level solution sets are compatible under reduction; compactness gives a compatible inverse-limit solution.That is exactly the structural pattern the sweep has been forcing.

1. Finite-level solution setsLetP=P1‚äÉP2‚äÉ‚čĮ be the complete G-stable pro-2 Magnus/Zassenhaus filtration of P. Thus each P/PN is a finite 2-group andN‚čāPN=1.SetFN:=F/PN‚ČÖ(P/PN)‚čäG.

For each N, define SN to be the set of over-G automorphismső¶N:FN‚ÜíFN such that:ő¶N(rS)=rH in¬†FN,up to replacing rS by rS‚ąí1, which does not change its closed normal closure; and such that the induced map on the first pro-2 Magnus layer is the fixed invertible H1 -matrix constructed in Turn 1.This is a finite set. Indeed, an over-G automorphism of FN is determined by finite data: an automorphism of the finite group P/PN, plus a continuous section twist G‚ÜíFN with values in finite P/PN -cosets. Since the kernel is finite, there are only finitely many such twists at level N.

2. Nonemptiness of every SN The previous turns establish exactly the ingredients needed to proveSNÓÄ†=‚ąÖfor¬†every¬†N.

First, Turn 1 computed the abelianized relation rowswS=(ŌÉ+3E,‚ąí2),wH=(Eg0+3E‚ąí2,ŌÉ‚ąíE),and constructed an idempotent-by-idempotent invertible matrix over őõ=Z2 [[G]] carrying wS to wH. On the (1‚ąíE)-piece the rows swap, and on the Ef-piece the matrix U0 has determinant s, a unit.Since P is free as a G-operator pro-2 group, this invertible H1 -data gives the initial Nielsen lift.

Second, after that H1 -change, all non-residual idempotent pieces are unit-controlled. The history records that the only dangerous piece is the residual Ef, mod-2, s=1 piece.Third, Turn 18 closes that residual tower to all orders. In residual notation,S(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)‚ąí1Y1‚ąí2Y03Y0q has target X2, and Turn 18 proves that for every N there are residual section-twisted lifts satisfyingS(Y0,Y1,q)‚Č°X2 (modIN).

The key uniform result wasCm=Lm (m‚Č•5),so every even square quotient is hit by a root transgression and every odd layer is an ordinary boundary.Therefore, for each fixed finite N, one may solve the full filtered Nielsen problem through level N: unit pieces are killed directly; the residual piece is killed by the all-order residual induction; and the section cocycle is included in the same finite calculation. ThusSNÓÄ†=‚ąÖ‚ąÄN.This avoids the mistake of freezing one finite-gauge representative. For each N, we are allowed to choose the lower-base and high-correction data best suited to level N.

3. Compact inverse-limit gluing

For M‚Č•N, reduction modulo PN gives a mapŌĀM,N:SM‚ÜíSN.

These maps are well-defined because equalityő¶M(rS)=rH in¬†FM reduces to equality in FN, and over-G automorphisms reduce to over-G automorphisms.Now use compactness. The sets SN are finite and nonempty. For any finite collection of levels, choose a solution at the largest level and reduce it to the smaller ones. Hence the inverse system has the finite-intersection property. ThereforeNlimSNÓÄ†=‚ąÖ.

Pick a compatible system(ő¶N)N‚ąąNlimSN.

Because

F‚ČÖNlimFN,the compatible finite-level automorphisms define a continuous automorphism ő¶:F‚ÜíF.

Each ő¶N is over G, so the inverse-limit automorphism is also over G. In particular,ŌÄ‚ąėő¶=ŌÄ on F.

Moreover, for every N,ő¶(rS)rH‚ąí1‚ąąPN.

Since ‚čāNPN=1, it follows thatő¶(rS)=rH in F, again up to the harmless inversion/conjugacy convention already noted in Turn 19. Turn 19 recorded the same normal-closure implication: once an over-G automorphism carries rS to rH, it carries the closed normal closure of rS to the closed normal closure of rH.Thus ő¶(‚ü®‚ü®rS‚ü©‚ü©F)=‚ü®‚ü®rH‚ü©‚ü©F.

Passing to quotients gives

F/‚ü®‚ü®rS‚ü©‚ü©F‚ąľ

F/‚ü®‚ü®rH‚ü©‚ü©F,i.e.őďS‚ąľőďH.Because ő¶ is over G, the induced quotient isomorphism satisfiesŌÄH‚ąėő¶=ŌÄS.

ThereforeőďS‚ČÖGőďH.

What changed from Turn 19

Turn 19‚Äôs conclusion was right, but its ‚ÄúHensel lift‚ÄĚ packaging was slightly too monotone. The safer theorem is:finite solvability at every Magnus level N, together with compactness of the finite-level over-G Nielsen solution sets, gives an exact inverse-limit over-G automorphism.This is better aligned with the actual obstruction pattern. Fixed-gauge tails repeatedly looked dangerous; the true invariant object was the full lower-plus-high torsor. The compactness argument lets each finite level choose the necessary lower-base retuning, then extracts a compatible global solution.

ESTABLISHED: For every finite Magnus level N, there exists a twisted over-G Nielsen automorphism of F/PN carrying rS to rH. The finite nonempty solution sets SN have compatible reduction maps, so compactness gives a limit automorphism ő¶:F‚ÜíF over G with ő¶(rS)=rH. Hence őďS‚ČÖGőďH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains, assuming the finite residual computations and the all-order induction from Turns 1‚Äď18 are accepted as established. What remains is only an audit/typesetting task: converting the sweep into a polished proof with the finite-level solution sets SN explicitly defined.

NEXT: Write the final standalone proof as a clean note: definitions, abelianized Nielsen matrix, residual induction theorem, finite-level compactness gluing, and quotient conclusion.[TURN20-COMPACTNESS-GLUING-ISO]
